articles.headline: Michigan tops Illinois for 1st outright Big Ten regular-season title since '14
articles.description: No. 3 Michigan defeated No. 10 Illinois 84-70 on Friday night and clinched the Big Ten regular-season title.

articles.headline: No. 3 Michigan claims Big Ten regular-season title with an 84-70 win over No. 10 Illinois
articles.description: — Morez Johnson Jr. had 19 points and 11 rebounds against his former team as No. 3 Michigan defeated No. 10 Illinois 84-70 on Friday night and clinched the Big Ten regular-season title.

articles.headline: Ndjonga's 18 help Canisius beat Merrimack 67-62

article.headline: No. 9 Kansas beats top-ranked and previously undefeated Arizona 82-78
article.description: — Flory Bidunga had a double-double and No. 9 Kansas defeated top-ranked and previously unbeaten Arizona 82-78 on Monday night.
article.linkText: No. 9 Kansas beats top-ranked and previously undefeated Arizona 82-78

images.name: Kansas hands Arizona its first loss of the season
images.caption: Ninth-ranked Kansas defeats No. 1 Arizona for the Wildcats' first loss of the season.

video.headline: Kansas hands Arizona its first loss of the season
video.shortHeadline: Kansas hands Arizona its first loss of the season
video.caption: Ninth-ranked Kansas defeats No. 1 Arizona for the Wildcats' first loss of the season.
video.title: Kansas hands Arizona its first loss of the season
video.description: Ninth-ranked Kansas defeats No. 1 Arizona for the Wildcats' first loss of the season.
video.shortDescription: Ninth-ranked Kansas defeats No. 1 Arizona for the Wildcats' first loss of the season.

tracking.trackingName: NCB_One-Play (Arizona Wildcats vs. Kansas Jayhawks: Game Highlights) 2026/02/10 ESHEET

article.story: LAWRENCE, Kan. -- — Flory Bidunga had a double-double and No. 9 Kansas defeated top-ranked and previously unbeaten Arizona 82-78 on Monday night. Arizona (23-1, 10-1 Big 12) entered Allen Fieldhouse with the second-longest winning streak in Division I men’s basketball. The loss ended a 23-game run for the Wildcats, second to Miami (Ohio), which has won 24 straight. Kansas (19-5, 9-2) played without freshman guard Darryn Peterson, who was a late scratch with flu-like symptoms. Peterson has missed 11 games with a combination of hamstring, calf and ankle issues. Arizona held a three-point lead at halftime and led by as many as 11 before Kansas took its first lead of the game at the 9:32 mark in the second half. Bidunga put the Jayhawks ahead with a layup, which capped a personal seven-point run. He finished with 23 points on 8-for-11 shooting and added 10 rebounds. Arizona closed within one point with 34 seconds left in the game, but senior guard Melvin Council Jr. shot 3 of 4 from the line in the final minute and 10 of 11 in the game to help seal the victory for Kansas. He finished with 23 points. Tre White also added a pair of late free throws. Four Kansas players scored double digits. Bryson Tiller finished with 18 points and Jamari McDowell added 10. Freshman guard Brayden Burries led Arizona with 25 points. Motiejus Krivas scored 14 and Ivan Kharchenkov 13. Up next Kansas: At Iowa State on Saturday. Arizona: Hosts Texas Tech on Saturday. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here and here (AP News mobile app). AP college basketball: https://apnews.com/hub/ap-top-25-college-basketball-poll and https://apnews.com/hub/college-basketball
